name: Acyclovir
executiveSummary: Disseminated herpes. Herpes zoster and varicella in the immunocompromised host. Herpetic encephalitis. Varicella in the newborn.
indications:
## Main indications

Disseminated herpes. Herpes zoster and varicella in the immunocompromised host. Herpetic encephalitis. Varicella in the newborn.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Lyophilized powder vial containing 500 mg (Lazar, LAFEDAR, LIA, Pharma Vial).
adulto.diluent: 500 mg in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl. If dose exceeds 500 mg, dilute in 250 mL 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Gently shake vial until solution is clear. Administer by slow infusion over more than 1 hour.
adulto.notes: Refrigeration of reconstituted solution (in vial) may cause precipitate that redissolves at room temperature. USE ONLY SWFI (other diluents may precipitate acyclovir).

pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 400–800 mg; Lyophilized powder vial: 500 mg; Suspension: 80 mg/mL; Cream/dermal ointment: 5%; Ophthalmic ointment: 3%
pediatrico.dose: IV: Varicella, herpes zoster: 30 mg/kg/day every 8 h. Herpetic encephalitis: < 12 years: 60 mg/kg/day every 8 h; > 12 years: 30 mg/kg/day every 8 h. Genital herpes, mucocutaneous herpes in immunocompromised host, herpetic gingivostomatitis: < 12 years: 30 mg/kg/day every 8 h; > 12 years: 15 mg/kg/day every 8 h. PO: 80 mg/kg/day every 6 h (maximum dose: 3,2 g), adults: 200–800 mg every 6–8 h. Dose in obese patients should be calculated based on ideal body weight. Bone marrow transplant prophylaxis: Herpes simplex, serology-positive patient (recipient and/or donor): 10 mg/kg/dose every 8 h or 250 mg/m²/dose every 8 h (from 24 h before conditioning until day +30; discontinue if ganciclovir is started). Unrelated allogeneic transplant recipients for CMV prophylaxis: 500 mg/m²/dose every 8 h. Dermatologic therapy: Herpes virus infection: apply 5 times daily for 1 week. Ophthalmology: 3% ophthalmic ointment every 3 h.
pediatrico.notes: Risk of phlebitis or injection-site inflammation at high concentrations (highly alkaline). Infuse over at least 1 hour at final concentration < 7 mg/mL. Adequate hydration is required to prevent acyclovir precipitation in renal tubules. Do not refrigerate reconstituted antibiotic.

neonatal.dose: 20 mg/kg/dose every 8 h in neonatal encephalitis.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 12 h at room temperature.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 12 h at room temperature.

## Pediatric guide

- Diluted with 5% dextrose: 24 h at room temperature.
- Diluted with SWFI: 12 h at room temperature.
- Do not refrigerate.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Headache, encephalopathic signs, hypotension, rash, pruritus, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, hematuria, arthralgia.
